fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Montana Respiratory Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a respiratory therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Montana - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: respiratory therapist license reciprocity montana, how to transfer respiratory therapist license to montana, respiratory therapist compact montana, montana respiratory therapist endorsement application

board.name: Montana Board of Respiratory Care Practitioners
board.mailing_address:
Montana Board of Medical Examiners
PO Box 200513
Helena MT 59620-0513

quick_facts.exam_name: National Board for Respiratory Care (NBRC) examinations

license_types.title: Registered Respiratory Therapist (RRT)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a respiratory therapist in Montana.

steps.title: Create an eLicense Account
steps.description: Navigate to the Montana eLicense portal at ebiz.mt.gov/POL and register for a new user account if you do not already have one.

steps.title: Complete Online Application
steps.description: Log into your new eLicense account, select the Respiratory Care Practitioner application option, and meticulously fill out all required sections of the online form, providing accurate personal, educational, and professional history.

steps.title: Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Submit the non-refundable application fee directly through the Montana eLicense system using an accepted payment method.

steps.title: Submit Supporting Documents
steps.description: Arrange for official transcripts to be sent directly from your educational institution, official verification of your NBRC credential (RRT or CRT) directly from NBRC, and official verifications of licensure from any other states where you hold or have held a license to be sent directly to the Board. Complete the required criminal background check process via fingerprinting.

steps.title: Monitor Application Status
steps.description: Regularly log into your Montana eLicense account to track the progress of your application and respond promptly to any requests for additional information or clarification from the Montana Board of Medical Examiners.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NBRC. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Montana respiratory therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Montana have reciprocity for respiratory therapists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers respiratory therapist practice in Montana. To work in Montana, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my respiratory therapist license to Montana?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Montana while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Respiratory Therapist in Montana
hero.dek: To apply for a Respiratory Therapist license in Montana, you must use the Montana eLicense online application system.

official_links.registration_steps: Go to ebiz.mt.gov/POL, click on 'Register A New Account' on the left side menu, and follow the prompts to create your user profile with your personal details and desired login credentials.
